role: Youth Soccer Team Mom (Weekend Tournament Coordinator)
platform: iOS (iPhone 13), occasionally the family iPad
budget_sensitivity: High — already resents TeamSnap's upsell banners; anything she pays for comes out of her own pocket, not team dues
primary_pain: Schedule changes buried in GroupMe and relayed across three channels; snack/canopy tasks never assigned; non-responding parents; keeping kids' info private
loved_most: The calendar day view and the invite-approval controls — 'someone here has met a forwarded link'
hated_most: Tasks (her #1 feature) buried at the far end of the mobile tab strip; convincing 13 families to create accounts
top_requested_feature: Read-only schedule view from the invite link without account creation
biggest_churn_risk: The third parent saying 'I'm not downloading another app,' or the first wrong/duplicated calendar entry

role: AAU Volleyball Parent Coordinator (Multi-City Tournament Season)
platform: Android (Pixel 8) — checks the Play Store before trusting any app
budget_sensitivity: Medium — won't subscribe annually for a seasonal problem, but a per-season price is rational to him
primary_pain: Mid-tournament bracket/schedule changes reaching only half the parents; announcement messages drowned by 60 emoji replies; hotel-block deadlines nobody meets
loved_most: Invite gatekeeping controls; the confirm-before-write AI design; calendar edit-in-place
hated_most: No Android app/Play Store presence, and tracked broadcasts (his killer feature) living in a $49 Pro tier; broken demo images and 401 console noise
top_requested_feature: Seen-by/delivery visibility on consumer-trip broadcasts and announcements
biggest_churn_risk: Android PWA push proving unreliable for a schedule change, or render jank making courtside use feel cheap

platform: iOS (iPhone 15 Pro), everything mobile-first
budget_sensitivity: Low for this trip — it's the wedding, money flows; but a recurring subscription for a one-weekend job is dead on arrival
primary_pain: Chasing 8 Venmo payments across 3 deposits; the group can't pick a dinner spot in under 200 messages; itinerary lives as an outdated JPEG
loved_most: The payments ledger with per-head math and pending/settled states; the live itinerary day view; the demo had a bachelorette trip in it
hated_most: The 3-split free cap ('I have three splits before the flights'); broken demo images; any rumor of jank
top_requested_feature: Pre-signup itinerary/cover teaser at the invite link so the slow bridesmaid can just see the plan
biggest_churn_risk: A bridesmaid saying the link 'didn't work' twice, or the app stuttering during the weekend in front of the girls

role: Guys' Golf Birthday Trip Planner (Atlanta → Scottsdale)
platform: iOS (iPhone 14)
budget_sensitivity: Medium — happily drops $300 on a round, deeply allergic to subscriptions; reviews his credit card for '$9.99/mo' parasites quarterly
primary_pain: Nobody responds to anything; the group decides nothing ('I'm easy' ×8); collecting $200/head happens in three painful waves
loved_most: The who-owes-who ledger with per-head math; polls with deadlines; expense events landing in chat where the shame lives
hated_most: The 3-split cap arriving exactly at house + two deposits; any whiff of monthly billing; AI offered where he needs human answers
top_requested_feature: Pre-auth read access — itinerary and active poll visible at the invite link before account creation
biggest_churn_risk: Three guys never join and the ledger only covers five-eighths of the money — at that point his Notes app is more accurate

platform: Mixed — Amanda iOS + desktop web (planning happens on a laptop); guests are iOS/Android/web/everything
group_size: 60 invited guests across 3 generations, ~15 in the wedding party with real logistics
budget_sensitivity: Low for the wedding line-item — happily pay real money once for something polished; zero appetite for the couple's tools becoming a subscription
primary_pain: 60 guests with wildly different tech ability who must know times/shuttles/dress codes; the same five questions asked sixty times; keeping grandma informed without an app
loved_most: The demo's event surfaces and the calendar day view — 'this is the welcome packet, alive'; first-event-free generosity; urgent broadcast styling
hated_most: The guest account wall — 'you built a B2C2C product and put a registration gate on the second C'
top_requested_feature: Tokenized read-only guest view (itinerary/agenda/places) at the invite link with no account required
biggest_churn_risk: The first test guest (Tyler's dad) failing to get past the invite preview — they'd know within one week of trying

platform: Android phone (large-font mode) + desktop-first for planning
budget_sensitivity: Very high — reunion money goes to the venue and the fish fry, not software; free only
primary_pain: Coordinating 35 relatives across four channels — nobody knows the latest version of the schedule and she is the information desk
loved_most: The polls page (the most elder-friendly screen in the app), the hotel/Base Camp directions page, and the honest demo-first landing
hated_most: Gray writing on a black screen everywhere with the light switch hidden in settings; the account wall in front of her elders
top_requested_feature: Read-only guest view for invite-link holders (plus free PDF printing) so low-tech elders can see the schedule without an account
biggest_churn_risk: The third relative calling saying 'it wants me to make an account' — that's the moment she posts the schedule back on Facebook

platform: iPhone only; laptop is for essays
budget_sensitivity: Extreme — broke, splits a $7 queso; free only, forever
primary_pain: 12 people, 3 commit early, 5 commit late, 4 ghost; collecting money; the Airbnb link dying in the iMessage scroll
loved_most: The demo-first landing (evaluated the whole product in 90 seconds with no email gate), polls with auto-close, and the Venmo deeplink
hated_most: Any group member hitting a signup wall; the '$1,000 trip' upsell copy; 3 free payment splits
top_requested_feature: Tokenized guest poll-voting and itinerary viewing from invite links — no account needed
biggest_churn_risk: Two friends fail to join from the link, OR an upgrade modal appears mid-trip — either one and they're back in iMessage like nothing happened

platform: iPhone 16 Pro (primary), MacBook for the planning doc
budget_sensitivity: Low for herself, moderate optics with the group; clear subscription candidate
primary_pain: Her curation lives in Notion where nobody comments; decisions decided too late for good reservations; the aesthetic chaos of juggling ugly apps
loved_most: The design system's discipline — landing to auth to trip without a single off-brand screen; Base Camp distances; confirm-before-write AI
hated_most: The mobile stutter she can feel; poll colors off-palette; broken demo images in a luxury pitch; the upgrade wall that points at settings instead of taking her money
top_requested_feature: Per-place notes/POV on saved places so her annotated curation maps onto the product
biggest_churn_risk: The AI returns the three most obvious tourist restaurants in Kyoto, or a friend screenshots a janky/broken-image moment into the group chat

platform: Mixed — Karen iPhone, Mike Android, planning on a shared iPad and Mike's work laptop
budget_sensitivity: Moderate — will pay for value but hate subscriptions on principle; prefer per-trip
primary_pain: Decision deadlock on where/when/budget, plus settling money couple-to-couple while every app thinks in individuals
loved_most: Polls with deadlines (Karen audibly said 'finally'), the house-as-Base-Camp distances, and the Trip Pass pricing model
hated_most: Explaining why the expense report shows Karen and Mike as separate debtors for the same household; the iPhone hiccups; off-screen mobile tabs
top_requested_feature: Household/couple grouping for payment splits ('split by couple: 4 ways across 8 people')
biggest_churn_risk: The first settlement round producing MORE confusion than Mike's spreadsheet — money is the trust moment; fumble it once and Excel wins forever

platform: iPhone + desktop (plans at a desk, executes from a phone)
group_size: 5-10 friends depending on the trip; 3-4 trips in flight at any time
budget_sensitivity: Low — pays for 9 SaaS tools personally; expects software to earn it
primary_pain: Chasing people — 'Did you book your flight?' x7; no way to know who's even SEEN the plan; he is the single point of failure for every trip
loved_most: The whole organizer toolkit in one place — tasks with assignment, polls that close themselves, group-aware AI with a confirm card, per-trip mute
hated_most: Tasks ping once at assignment and then go silent forever; the invite funnel three steps longer than Partiful; 'voice concierge' meaning dictation
top_requested_feature: Automated task reminders with escalation (T-7/T-3/T-1, overdue pings to the assignee) plus 'seen by' on plans and broadcasts
biggest_churn_risk: Two of eight friends stall at signup — the moment the tool needs HIM to chase people INTO it, it has reproduced his problem with extra steps

platform: Desktop-first (two monitors) + iPhone on game weekends
budget_sensitivity: Low at the price level, high at the procurement level — legal, security review, and an MSA required
primary_pain: Last-minute manifest changes and proving 180 people who don't read email were notified of gate/bus/lobby changes, reconciled across four spreadsheets
loved_most: The Pro demo's information architecture — roster with roles, org chart, channels split by function, broadcasts with urgency styling
hated_most: Realizing the entire ops layer (roster, rooms, schedule, per-diem, medical, compliance) is empty arrays on real trips — evaluated a movie set
top_requested_feature: CSV/manifest import populating roster + rooms + schedule on real Pro trips, plus SSO and audit logs
biggest_churn_risk: The first real trip he creates has none of what the demo showed — abandons immediately

platform: iPhone (travel days) + laptop (planning days)
budget_sensitivity: Medium — can expense $99/mo on a department card without drama; bigger commitments need her VP
primary_pain: Schedule churn ripples — one flex game means new charter slot, hotel dates, bus times, per-diem day count — plus telling 45 people the bus moved and knowing who saw it
loved_most: Broadcasts vs Channels vs Messages as separate streams with unread counts — her comms doctrine, productized; and per-trip mute actually existing
hated_most: Hitting 'start trial' intent and getting a mailto link; the demo erroring 'Couldn't load trip members' mid-pitch
top_requested_feature: Schedule + rooming-list import (ICS/CSV/PDF) populating real Pro trip ops fields
biggest_churn_risk: Creating the real road-swing trip and facing hand-typing 4 cities × 45 people of schedule/rooms

role: University Basketball Travel Coordinator (Director of Basketball Operations, Duke)
platform: iPhone primarily; office laptop for compliance paperwork
group_size: ~25 travel party (15 players, coaches, trainers, managers, SID) + compliance officers
budget_sensitivity: Medium-high — not his money but a PO process; no credit-card self-serve, ever; athletic department procurement + counsel addendum
primary_pain: Producing per-diem and travel records for compliance audits, with duplicate data entry between Teamworks, Concur, and compliance docs
loved_most: The Team tab — roles, org chart, approval queue, dietary fields — and the PDF Recap's privacy sentence; both read like someone interviewed an ops director
hated_most: '1 Free Export per trip' metering on the one artifact he must produce repeatedly; a mailto link standing where a procurement process should be
top_requested_feature: Per-diem/compliance data layer with audit-template PDF export
biggest_churn_risk: First compliance audit cycle where he still rebuilds everything in Excel — the app becomes a seventh tool instead of replacing two

platform: Desktop-first (school office PC); personal iPhone for sideline emergencies
group_size: 8 teams × ~20 students + 2-3 coaches each + parents on the comms tree
budget_sensitivity: Extreme — a $49/month subscription is a school-board line item; anything above free-ish needs a requisition, a vote, and a fiscal-year cycle
primary_pain: Parents not getting bus-time changes, chasing permission slips, emergency contacts in a binder, and every coach communicating differently
loved_most: The broadcast stream — 'that's Remind but inside the schedule app'; overall polish nicer than anything the district uses
hated_most: Being named as a category ('School') and then priced like a touring rock band; 'Free works forever' until you need the one feature that matters
top_requested_feature: A Community/School SKU with broadcasts, read-only parent membership, multi-team trips — plus a permission-slip feature
biggest_churn_risk: The pilot team's parents hit the account wall and half never join — back to email for those families means email for everyone

role: Tour Manager for a Touring Comedian
platform: iPhone only — the laptop lives in the backpack and comes out for settlements, maybe
group_size: Party of 6 — comedian, opener, Jamie, merch person, occasional security, occasional photographer
budget_sensitivity: High but pragmatic — $49/mo from tour budget if it demonstrably kills the spreadsheet; prefers per-tour pricing because tours are lumpy (8 weeks on, 6 off)
primary_pain: Re-typing venue advance PDFs into the day sheet; 'what time is lobby call' asked six times a day; changes propagating to nobody; everything living in Jamie's head and phone
loved_most: The demo data's fluency — 'Tour Manager' role chips, #production channel, 'Showtime in 90 minutes' broadcast; somebody on this team has done an advance
hated_most: Falling for the demo and then reading `schedule: []`; the trial button opening Mail.app on the phone
top_requested_feature: Venue advance PDF import that builds the real-trip day sheet, plus a 'duplicate trip structure for next leg' button
biggest_churn_risk: Night one of a real leg, when building the day sheet means re-typing the venue PDF into Chravel instead of into Sheets — same work, new app, no

platform: iPhone in hand, iPad at the production desk
group_size: 80-person traveling party — band, crew, security, plus local promoter reps per city
budget_sensitivity: Low for tools that work — tour budget absorbs $99/mo without a meeting; zero tolerance for tools that fail on show day
primary_pain: Rooming-list churn, crew who never read the day sheet, settlement night chaos, and six apps for one show day
loved_most: The Beyoncé Pro demo — role-targeted broadcasts, channels, and the PDF recap privacy defaults; someone on this team understands touring
hated_most: Real Pro trips initialize roster/rooms/schedule/settlement empty after the demo promised all of them; half-built settlement money surfaces
top_requested_feature: Ops onboarding for real Pro trips — rooming-list CSV import/export, schedule builder, populated editable ops surfaces
biggest_churn_risk: Builds the real tour, sees empty ops surfaces, and keeps the shadow spreadsheet anyway — or the first time settlement math is wrong

platform: MacBook in the production office, iPhone on set
budget_sensitivity: Production buys tools per show; $49-99/mo is petty cash IF it replaces a line item
primary_pain: 11pm schedule revisions fan out across five channels; confirming receipt of a revised call sheet; union turnaround math done by hand; dead zones at remote locations
loved_most: Smart Import's preview/cherry-pick design; Productions existing as a first-class category; directions inline in Places
hated_most: Free-tier quota chrome on Pro surfaces; single-basecamp model on a 3-location shoot; no offline guarantee for today's schedule
top_requested_feature: A "pin this day offline" toggle with a cached-at timestamp on top of the existing service worker
biggest_churn_risk: An import mangles or duplicates one call time on a shared calendar — a set-wide incident, and the idempotency gap is documented

platform: Desktop-first Chrome power user, iPhone secondary
budget_sensitivity: Medium — needs invoicing and procurement approval, not credit cards; line item must survive finance review
primary_pain: Collecting structured info (dietary, flight numbers, room shares) from 150 people; RSVP tracking; comms nobody reads; stitching forms→sheets→slack by hand
loved_most: The corporate retreat demo being a real scenario; broadcast delivery tracking; PDF recap privacy defaults
hated_most: No structured intake forms and no SSO — and re-explaining to every vendor that she needs SSO and an invoice
top_requested_feature: Structured attendee intake forms (custom fields, required/optional, completion dashboard)
biggest_churn_risk: The retreat intake still requires Google Forms — at that point Chravel is a fourth tool, not a consolidation

platform: Desktop Outlook/Concur mastery, iPhone always
budget_sensitivity: Price-insensitive, trust-sensitive — client-billable/company card; one privacy incident costs more than any subscription
primary_pain: Itinerary leaks (forwarded PDFs), version drift across principals' copies, coordinating drivers/security without exposing the full picture, after-hours changes
loved_most: Containment defaults — guest zero-access, viewer read-only, "Emails and phone numbers hidden" recap default, Private personal basecamp
hated_most: The invite preview showing trip details to anyone holding a link; AI on by default with group context and no visible per-trip off switch
top_requested_feature: Confidential-trip mode: no link previews, admin-only roster visibility, AI off, invite by direct named grant only
biggest_churn_risk: Seeing a trip name render on an unauthenticated screen, or reading the wildcard-CORS/demo-super-admin findings — instant, permanent abandonment

platform: Desktop-first operator
group_size: Per client trip 2-20; portfolio of ~15 active trips at any time
budget_sensitivity: Low-medium — $99+/mo is fine IF it's client-facing polished; he resells the experience at margin
primary_pain: Itinerary tools that look like the tool's brand instead of his; juggling 15 trips across tabs; clients asking questions answered on page 2; per-client billing chaos
loved_most: The PDF pipeline being real with privacy defaults — and the code comment about client-facing deliverables; the dark-gold aesthetic he could almost pass off as his own
hated_most: "Made with Chravel" on his deliverable with the customization hook sitting unexposed in code; 3-trip cap blocking a realistic trial; no checkout for Pro
top_requested_feature: White-label client view (logo, colors, footer) plus a 15-trip portfolio command dashboard
biggest_churn_risk: A client asks "what's Chravel?" one too many times — brand dilution is a slow leak measurable in rebookings

platform: iPhone 15 Pro in the field, MacBook Air at the desk
group_size: 8-15 vendors + couple + wedding party (~20 working people), 100-200 guests downstream
budget_sensitivity: Low for tools that bill to clients; allergic to per-seat pricing for 200 guests
primary_pain: Vendors who don't read email; day-of timeline changes that never reach the band; couples re-asking the same timing questions
loved_most: The Agenda tab — her run sheet, live, on every phone
hated_most: Names-only team chips; 'Frequent Chraveler' being the upgrade she's told to buy; no vendor roles
top_requested_feature: Vendor-tier event roles (vendor / wedding party / guest) with scoped chat and agenda visibility
biggest_churn_risk: The 4th event paywall hitting mid-pilot, or the first time a guest sees vendor chat

platform: Desktop-first (Windows + Chrome); iPhone secondary
budget_sensitivity: Medium — has budget, but procurement demands reliability evidence and references
primary_pain: Session-change comms at scale, attendee-app costs ($8-15k/yr), and staff/volunteer coordination split from attendee comms
loved_most: The agenda's scheduled/unscheduled model and Upload Schedule — someone on this team has run an event
hated_most: Having to read audit docs to learn the notification system can't survive his event — the site implies readiness it doesn't have
top_requested_feature: Async/batched notification fanout plus enforced, documented attendee caps
biggest_churn_risk: First broadcast to a large event visibly stalls or double-sends; or enforcement of caps arrives mid-event

platform: iPhone 14, exclusively; opens a laptop only at work
group_size: 15 friends at a 3-day music festival (two Airbnbs, one rental van)
budget_sensitivity: High — she'll pay $6 for oat milk but interrogates every subscription
primary_pain: "Where ARE you guys" texts at the festival; lineup-clash debates buried in 400 unread messages; photos scattered across 15 camera rolls
loved_most: A Line-up tab existing at all, and the mobile agenda cards — "this was made by someone who goes to things"
hated_most: Her friends needing full accounts to see a schedule she made for them; far tabs hiding behind a strip-scroll; 3-splits free cap on a 15-person trip
top_requested_feature: Guest/RSVP-light mode — read agenda, pins, polls before account creation
biggest_churn_risk: Three friends text her "it's making me sign up, just screenshot it" — the moment Chravel becomes a second chat instead of the chat

platform: Android (Pixel 7) + desktop at home for "real work"
group_size: 40 runners for race weekend (carpools, one hotel block, three pace groups)
budget_sensitivity: Medium-high personally; the club has dues (~$30/yr × 120) and a small treasury
primary_pain: Race-morning chaos — wave times, gear drop, parking; carpool musical chairs; the same five questions asked forty times
loved_most: Hero copy naming community clubs, and the calendar Export button — someone thought about people like her
hated_most: Being a volunteer staring at a $19.99/mo personal subscription named after frequent travel
top_requested_feature: A club/organization SKU — one payer, many members, a few events a year
biggest_churn_risk: The third member texts "it won't let me in" on race morning, or event #4 demands her credit card

platform: iPhone 15, exclusively; touches a laptop only for class
group_size: 120 PNMs (potential new members) + 80 active brothers; events daily for ~3 weeks
budget_sensitivity: Personally broke; chapter treasury routinely approves $100-300 for rush tools/supplies
primary_pain: Daily schedule changes that half the chapter misses; "what time is it at" ×50; knowing who actually saw the announcement; PNM attendance tracking on paper
loved_most: The agenda cards and the fact that the demo literally ships a fraternity trip with approval-required invites — someone gets my world
hated_most: Tab-strip hide-and-seek; no read counts visible in the event demo; the idea of paying $19.99/mo personally
top_requested_feature: Broadcast read counts ("147 of 200 seen") plus bulk-approve for join requests
biggest_churn_risk: The first venue-change blast visibly doesn't land — stragglers at the wrong place ends the experiment same-day; or the mute-spiral makes it GroupMe #4

role: Church & nonprofit group trip organizer (mission trip + youth retreat)
platform: Android + desktop
budget_sensitivity: Extreme — church funds, invoice/check only
primary_pain: Forms/waivers for minors and reaching low-tech members are unserved; payments can't model installments or confidential scholarships
loved_most: Calendar as single schedule of record; require-approval invites
hated_most: Account wall excluding elderly members; no forms feature at all
top_requested_feature: Per-member forms/waivers with completion tracking
biggest_churn_risk: Members fail to join via link and the group stays on SMS

role: School field trip coordinator (60 students + 8 chaperones)
platform: Desktop-first (district Chromebook) + personal iPhone
budget_sensitivity: Absolute — district procurement; free or nothing
primary_pain: Reaching 120 parents one-way without student accounts; chaperone coordination off a paper binder
loved_most: Tasks + calendar matching her checklist workflow; approval-gated invites
hated_most: The broadcast tool she needs is on a tier she can never buy; no education privacy posture
top_requested_feature: No-account read-only parent view (calendar + announcements)
biggest_churn_risk: Compliance office asks for a student-data answer and there is nothing to forward

role: Bachelor party planner / group CFO (11 guys, Boston to Montreal)
platform: iPhone (web/PWA)
budget_sensitivity: Price-sensitive on software, free-spending on the trip itself
primary_pain: Collecting deposits and splitting uneven bills across 11 guys with one chronic non-payer
loved_most: Payments tab — You Owe/You're Owed/Net summary, pending vs settled, Venmo deeplink
hated_most: 3-split free cap colliding with expense #4 mid-trip, with a $39.99 escape hatch
top_requested_feature: A $10-15 weekend pass
biggest_churn_risk: Split-limit modal at the steakhouse sends the group back to Splitwise in 90 seconds

role: Luxury travel advisor serving affluent clients (~30 trips/year)
platform: Desktop-first + iPhone
budget_sensitivity: Low for client-facing tools; zero tolerance for brand dilution
primary_pain: Client-facing brand mismatch — 'The Group Chat Travel App' cannot greet a $90,000 commission
loved_most: PDF Recap privacy engineering and Pro roster/broadcast architecture
hated_most: No white-label at any tier; emailing billing@ to spend money
top_requested_feature: White-label branding incl. custom invite domain and branded export
biggest_churn_risk: First client screenshot showing Chravel's logo where hers should be

role: International multi-country reunion organizer (18 people, 5 countries, 3 languages)
platform: Android (Pixel) + desktop
budget_sensitivity: Medium — pays per-trip, allergic to subscriptions for triennial events
primary_pain: Timezone ambiguity, three languages, and multi-currency chaos handled across 6-7 disconnected apps
loved_most: Visible engineering care in date handling; currency model with per-currency decimals; timezone-aware inputs
hated_most: No i18n layer at all — not incomplete, absent
top_requested_feature: UI localization plus viewer-local time rendering with explicit zone labels
biggest_churn_risk: First ambiguously rendered cross-timezone event kills the family's calendar trust in one WhatsApp message
